description: Defensive / surface-level session. Client deflects with humor and intellectualization, never goes to the body or the felt sense. Expected: NO shifts, NO breakthroughs (rubric demands evidence ≥7 with a clear contradicted-pattern moment — none here), session_themes captures the surface-stay direction='stuck', no trauma flag.

messages.content: Good to see you. What's most alive for you today?
Nothing in particular. Same week as always — too much to do, not enough hours, the usual.
When you say 'the usual,' what comes up underneath that?
Ha. Underneath? Probably more spreadsheets. I'm a project manager, that's pretty much the answer.
I notice the joke. Is there something you'd want to talk about if we slowed down a bit?
I mean, I could complain about my boss. Standard issue. Nothing dramatic.
What's the standard issue with your boss?
She micromanages. I roll my eyes. We move on. It's fine. I just have to remind myself that work is work.
When you remind yourself work is work, what happens in your body?
Nothing? I'm not really a body person. I'm pretty much always in my head.
Mm. If your head was the only one in the room and the rest of you got a turn — what might that part want to say?
Probably 'go take a nap.' That's about as deep as I get.
Sometimes the answer is the nap. I don't want to push if you're not in a place to go further today. Is there anything else worth naming before we close?
Not really. I think I'm good. Just needed a break from the spreadsheets honestly.
Okay. Thanks for showing up. We'll be here next time.
